title: Status, Events, Logs, Metrics, Traces, Audit, and Layered Incident Response · Control-Plane Trace: K8s Layered Debugging Method
summary: A release has green desired replicas but user failures, overwritten events, restarted containers, missing cluster logs, high-cardinality metrics, and no cross-layer correlation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s layered debugging method increment in cluster and workload incident evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Diagnose desired state, admission, scheduling, image, mount, process, probe, endpoint, DNS, policy, routing, storage, node, and application layers using the least invasive evidence.
Correlate request IDs, trace context, API audit events, release identity, workload logs, metrics, and policy decisions without leaking secrets or high-cardinality labels.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Layered Debugging Method · Theory K8s Layered Debugging Method
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory workload restarts after revision 3; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Layered Debugging Method contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example workload restarts after revision 3; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline. Competency probe: Diagnose desired state, admission, scheduling, image, mount, process, probe, endpoint, DNS, policy, routing, storage, node, and application layers using the least invasive evidence. A defensible solution applies K8s Layered Debugging Method, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cluster and workload incident evidence record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-layered-debugging-method — Diagnose desired state, admission, scheduling, image, mount, process, probe, endpoint, DNS, policy, routing, storage, node, and application layers using the least invasive evidence.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-observability-debugging-incidents-2
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-observability-debugging-incidents-2
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked k8s observability debugging incidents 2
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      correlation: release-uid-request-trace
      status: generation-and-conditions
      events: timestamped-short-retention
      logs: current-previous-and-node
      metrics: resource-system-application
      audit: identity-verb-resource
      diagnosis: first-causal-layer
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pod,endpointslice -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous --timestamps
    failure: Reject green replica count as user proof, restart as diagnosis, events as durable history, kubectl logs as archival storage, or unbounded cardinality.
    repair: Preserve identity and time, correlate desired and observed layers, reproduce one fault cleanly, retain the regression, and document telemetry limits.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Restarting or exec-editing the failing Pod is the fastest reliable root-cause method. This fails the K8s Layered Debugging Method contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
